acts.title: Public Examinations Act
acts.summary: Principal legislation for the conduct of public examinations (O/L, A/L, government examinations). Establishes the Commissioner of Examinations as administrative head, defines question papers as secret documents, and prescribes punishments for examination offences including impersonation, cheating, and divulging information.

acts.title: Assisted Schools and Training Colleges (Special Provisions) Act
acts.summary: Principal instrument for the 1960s nationalisation of denominational and semi-private (Assisted) schools. Empowered the Minister to appoint the Director of Education as school manager, with proprietors given the option to continue as strictly 'unaided' schools. Supplemented by Act No. 8 of 1961 (property vesting) and amended by Act No. 65 of 1981 (financial relief for unaided schools). Currently under Law Commission review.

acts.title: National Library and Documentation Services Board Act
acts.summary: Establishes the National Library and Documentation Services Board as a body corporate to govern library and documentation services nationwide. Creates the physical National Library Centre in Colombo, appoints a Director-General as principal executive, and provides a comprehensive financial framework. Replaced the 1970 Act. Never amended in over 25 years; currently under SLLA review.

acts.title: School Development Boards Act
acts.summary: Mandates a School Development Board for every school in Sri Lanka, formalizing parental and community involvement in school management. Principal serves as Chairman alongside teachers, parents, alumni, and well-wishers. Establishes a School Development Fund per school. Never amended in over 30 years.

acts.title: UNESCO Scholarship Fund Act
acts.summary: Establishes the UNESCO Scholarship Fund as a body corporate to provide scholarships for disabled and displaced children. Board of Management composed entirely of senior government officials chaired by Secretary to Ministry of Education. Also funds educational book publishing. Never amended since 1999.

acts.title: State Printing Corporation Act
acts.summary: Established the State Printing Corporation as a body corporate to serve as the chief printing and publication arm of the government. Originally focused on government printing and school textbooks, progressively expanded through three amendments (1978, 1981, 1998) to include commercial printing, import/export of books, and stationery manufacture. 32 sections across 4 parts.

acts.title: Education Ordinance
acts.summary: Foundational education law of Sri Lanka, enacted during the colonial period. Established the Department of Education, advisory councils, and codified rules for school management. 63 sections across 7 parts. Amended 4 times (1947 Free Education, 1951, 1958, 1973). Operational framework modified by the 13th Constitutional Amendment (1987 Provincial Council devolution).
